rights_summary.source_policy: NASA/JPL/Cassini public mission imagery plus NASA Apollo public-domain Earth imagery only; no paid stock, no credit-spend generation, no uploads/publishing.
rights_summary.nasa_media_note: NASA imagery is generally not copyrighted in the United States, but NASA names/insignia/logos may not imply endorsement. Plates must avoid NASA logos, labels, mission patches, UI, watermarks, and source-page text.
rights_summary.credit_note_for_internal_docs: Credit: NASA/JPL-Caltech/Space Science Institute for Cassini imagery and NASA/Apollo 17 for the Earth proxy where appropriate in descriptions/metadata, not burned into visual plates.

sources.title: Over Saturn's Turbulent North
sources.rights_notes: Free/public NASA/JPL Cassini image candidate; do not show NASA logo/insignia; no burned-in credit/text in plate.
sources.zero_text_notes: Raw planetary image file; no visible labels/logos/watermarks expected in the image pixels. Must still receive OCR/manual artifact pass after crop/enhance.

supports_beats.reason: North-pole hexagon can be cropped for immediate six-sided storm read.
NASA/Cassini proof plate for real jet stream around north pole.
Packet identifies this source for each-side-about-Earth-width scale proof; use a local non-text Earth proxy and bracket only after plate QC.
Central circular storm/polar area visible; may support inside-spinning beat if crop reads clearly.
Final pullback can show hexagon as cloud/weather structure on Saturn.

sources.current_limitations: 1024px source; must be tested at phone-size vertical crop. Scale overlay not yet built.

sources.title: Stormy North
sources.rights_notes: Free/public NASA/JPL Cassini image candidate; no NASA logo/insignia; no burned-in credit/text in plate.
sources.zero_text_notes: Raw planetary image file; no visible labels/logos/watermarks expected in the image pixels. Must still receive OCR/manual artifact pass after crop/enhance.

supports_beats.reason: Documentary north-pole source within the hexagon.
Best inside-hexagon storm/vortex detail source for spinning-weather beat.
Can supply close weather texture for final composite if not overused.

sources.current_limitations: May not show full hexagon boundary strongly enough for opening/scale without pairing with PIA21052.

sources.title: Hexagon and Rings
sources.rights_notes: Free/public NASA/JPL Cassini image candidate; no NASA logo/insignia; no burned-in credit/text in plate.
sources.zero_text_notes: Raw planetary image file; no visible labels/logos/watermarks expected in the image pixels. Must still receive OCR/manual artifact pass after crop/enhance.

supports_beats.reason: Hexagon plus rings can establish Saturn if the hexagon remains readable at phone size.
Possible final pullback connecting familiar rings to polar hexagon/weather.

sources.current_limitations: Opening hard-fails if the hexagon is too small/soft after vertical crop; likely secondary only.

sources.title: Saturn Watercolor Swirls
sources.rights_notes: Free/public NASA/JPL Cassini image candidate; no NASA logo/insignia; no burned-in credit/text in plate.
sources.zero_text_notes: Raw planetary image file; no visible labels/logos/watermarks expected in the image pixels. Must still receive OCR/manual artifact pass after crop/enhance.

supports_beats.reason: Atmospheric swirl texture may support weather feel but should not be primary proof.
Can support premium weather/cloud look if grounded by stronger hexagon source.

sources.current_limitations: Not primary proof for hexagon; use only if it passes phone-size weather/hexagon read after crop.

sources.title: View of the Earth seen by the Apollo 17 crew traveling toward the moon (Blue Marble)
sources.rights_notes: Free/right-safe NASA Apollo public-domain image candidate from NASA Images; no paid stock, credit-spend generation, upload, logo, insignia, or endorsement lane used. Credit NASA/Apollo 17 in internal metadata/descriptions only, not burned into plate pixels.
sources.zero_text_notes: Full-disk Earth photograph only; no visible captions, labels, UI, logos, watermarks, or source-page text expected in the image pixels. Use as a tiny unlabeled Earth scale proxy for Shot 03; final composite still needs OCR/manual artifact pass after crop/mask/scale.

supports_beats.reason: Clean full-disk Earth source suitable for compositing as a small unlabeled Earth comparison beside/bracketed against one Saturn hexagon side.

sources.current_limitations: Acquisition only: Earth proxy is downloaded but not masked/rescaled into a finished Shot 03 plate; final side bracket, phone-size readability, and OCR/manual zero-text QC remain downstream.
